title: Race-year grouped validation and model selection
summary: The current holdout is random stratified, but lap data are autocorrelated within races and drivers.
rationale: The current holdout is random stratified, but lap data are autocorrelated within races and drivers. Scikit-learn warns that standard random CV is inappropriate for time-dependent samples because nearby observations violate independence, so local AUC may overestimate public performance or choose brittle features.
implementation_hint: Evaluate candidate feature sets with GroupKFold by Race-Year or leave-one-race/year-out folds, optionally preserving chronology by training on earlier years and validating later years. Use this validation only for feature/model selection, then retrain the chosen configuration on full train for submission.
expected_effect: May reduce misleading local gains and identify features that generalize across races instead of memorizing event-specific pit windows.
risk: If Kaggle test is randomly sampled from the same races as train, stricter grouped CV may under-rank useful race-specific encodings; compare both random and grouped CV before discarding ideas.
